title: Equations with variables on both sides
description: Practice solving one-variable equations like 20 - 7x = 6x - 6, where the variable appears on both sides of the equals sign.
topic_path: Topic A: Writing and solving linear equations
Equations with variables on both sides

hints: We need to manipulate the equation to get $   b    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 4b+5&=1+5b \\\\
4b+5 \blueD{-5b} &= 1+5b\blueD{-5b} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract }5b\text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-b+5&=1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-b+5\blueD{-5} &= 1\blueD{-5} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract }5\text{ from each side.}}\\\\
-b &=-4 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-b}{\blueD{-1}} &= \dfrac{-4}{\blueD{-1}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by }-1\text{.}}\\\\
b &= \greenD{4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$b = \greenD{  4     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $   d    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 2d+4&=10+5d \\\\
2d+4 \blueD{-5d} &= 10+5d\blueD{-5d} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $5d$ from each side.}}\\\\
-3d+4&=10 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-3d+4\blueD{-4} &= 10\blueD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $4$ from each side.}}\\\\
-3d &=6 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-3d}{\blueD{-3}} &= \dfrac{6}{\blueD{-3}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-3$.}}\\\\
d &= \greenD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$d = \greenD{  -2     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    b   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 7b-15&=5b-3 \\\\
7b-15 \blueD{-5b} &= 5b-3\blueD{-5b} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $5b$ from each side.}}\\\\
2b-15&=-3 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2b-15\blueD{+15} &= -3\blueD{+15} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $15$ to each side.}}\\\\
2b &=12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{2b}{\blueD{2}} &= \dfrac{12}{\blueD{2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $2$.}}\\\\
b &= \greenD{6} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ b= \greenD{   6    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $   d    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 4d-4&=5d-8 \\\\
4d-4 \blueD{-5d} &= 5d-8\blueD{-5d} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $5d$ from each side.}}\\\\
-1d-4&=-8 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-1d-4\blueD{+4} &= -8\blueD{+4} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $4$ to each side.}}\\\\
-1d &=-4 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-1d}{\blueD{-1}} &= \dfrac{-4}{\blueD{-1}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-1$.}}\\\\
d &= \greenD{4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ d= \greenD{   4    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $   n    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 2-2n&=3n+17 \\\\
2-2n \blueD{-3n} &= 3n+17\blueD{-3n} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $3n$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5n+2&=17 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-5n+2\blueD{-2} &= 17\blueD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $2$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5n &=15 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-5n}{\blueD{-5}} &= \dfrac{15}{\blueD{-5}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-5$.}}\\\\
n &= \greenD{-3} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ n= \greenD{    -3   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $s       $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 8-4s&=s+13 \\\\
8-4s \blueD{-s} &= s+13\blueD{-s} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $s$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5s+8&=13 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-5s+8\blueD{-8} &= 13\blueD{-8} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $8$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5s &=5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-5s}{\blueD{-5}} &= \dfrac{5}{\blueD{-5}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-5$.}}\\\\
s &= \greenD{-1} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$s = \greenD{ -1      }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $a$ by itself.

$\begin{align} 5 + 14a &= 9a - 5 \\\\
5 + 14a \blueD{- 9a} &= 9a - 5 \blueD{- 9a} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $9a$ from each side.}}\\\\
5 + 5a &= -5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
5 + 5a \blueD{-5} &= -5 \blueD{- 5} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $5$ from each side.}}\\\\
5a &= -10 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{5a}{\blueD5} &= \dfrac{-10}{\blueD5} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $5$.}}\\\\
a &= \greenD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$a = \greenD{-2}~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    a   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} a-15&=4a-3 \\\\
a-15 \blueD{-4a} &= 4a-3\blueD{-4a} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $4a$ from each side.}}\\\\
-3a-15&=-3 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-3a-15\blueD{+15} &= -3\blueD{+15} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $15$ to each side.}}\\\\
-3a &=12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-3a}{\blueD{-3}} &= \dfrac{12}{\blueD{-3}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-3$.}}\\\\
a &= \greenD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ a= \greenD{   -4    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $  e     $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 9e-7&=7e-11 \\\\
9e-7 \blueD{-7e} &= 7e-11\blueD{-7e} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $7e$ from each side.}}\\\\
2e-7&=-11 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2e-7\blueD{+7} &= -11\blueD{+7} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $7$ to each side.}}\\\\
2e &=-4 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{2e}{\blueD{2}} &= \dfrac{-4}{\blueD{2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $2$.}}\\\\
e &= \greenD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$e = \greenD{    -2   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $     c  $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 12c-4&=14c-10 \\\\
12c-4 \blueD{-14c} &= 14c-10\blueD{-14c} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $14c$ from each side.}}\\\\
-2c-4&=-10 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-2c-4\blueD{+4} &= -10\blueD{+4} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $4$ to each side.}}\\\\
-2c &=-6 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-2c}{\blueD{-2}} &= \dfrac{-6}{\blueD{-2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-2$.}}\\\\
c &= \greenD{3} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}\\\\
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ c= \greenD{    3   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    g   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} -3+5+6g&=11-3g\\\\
2+6g&=11-3g &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2+6g \blueD{+3g} &= 11-3g\blueD{+3g} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $3g$ to each side.}}\\\\
9g+2&=11 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
9g+2\blueD{-2} &= 11\blueD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $2$ from each side.}}\\\\
9g &=9 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{9g}{\blueD{9}} &= \dfrac{9}{\blueD{9}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $9$.}}\\\\
g &= \greenD{1} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$g = \greenD{     1  }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $   f    $ by itself.

$\begin{align} -f+2+4f&=8-3f\\\\
2+3f&=8-3f &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2+3f \blueD{+3f} &= 8-3f\blueD{+3f} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $3f$ to each side.}}\\\\
6f+2&=8 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
6f+2\blueD{-2} &= 8\blueD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $2$ from each side.}}\\\\
6f &=6 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{6f}{\blueD{6}} &= \dfrac{6}{\blueD{6}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $6$.}}\\\\
f &= \greenD{1} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$f = \greenD{ 1      }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    m   $ by itself.

$\begin{align}-7+4m+10&=15-2m\\\\
4m+3&=15-2m&&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
3+4m \blueD{+2m} &= 15-2m\blueD{+2m} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $2m$ to each side.}}\\\\
6m+3&=15 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
6m+3\blueD{-3} &= 15\blueD{-3} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $3$ from each side.}}\\\\
6m &=12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{6m}{\blueD{6}} &= \dfrac{12}{\blueD{6}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $6$.}}\\\\
m &= \greenD{2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$m = \greenD{  2     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    h   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 17+4h+2&=1-5h \\\\
19+4h&=1-5h &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
19+4h \blueD{+5h} &= 1-5h\blueD{+5h} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $5h$ to each side.}}\\\\
9h+19&=1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
9h+19\blueD{-19} &= 1\blueD{-19} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $19$ from each side.}}\\\\
9h &=-18 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{9h}{\blueD{9}} &= \dfrac{-18}{\blueD{9}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $9$.}}\\\\
h &= \greenD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$h = \greenD{  -2     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    k   $ by itself.

$\begin{align}20-6+4k&=2-2k \\\\ 14+4k&=2-2k &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
14+4k \blueD{+2k} &= 2-2k\blueD{+2k} &&\small\gray{\text{Add $2k$ to each side.}}\\\\
6k+14&=2 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
6k+14\blueD{-14} &= 2\blueD{-14} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $14$ from each side.}}\\\\
6k &=-12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{6k}{\blueD{6}} &= \dfrac{-12}{\blueD{6}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $6$.}}\\\\
k &= \greenD{-2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$k = \greenD{   -2    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    r   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 16-2r&=-3r+6r+1 \\\\
 16-2r&=3r+1&&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
16-2r \blueD{-3r} &= 3r+1\blueD{-3r} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $3r$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5r+16&=1 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-5r+16\blueD{-16} &= 1\blueD{-16} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $16$ from each side.}}\\\\
-5r &=-15 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-5r}{\blueD{-5}} &= \dfrac{-15}{\blueD{-5}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-5$.}}\\\\
r &= \greenD{3} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ r= \greenD{   3    }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $  e     $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 9e+4&=-5e+14+13e\\\\
9e+4&=14+8e &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
9e+4 \blueD{-8e} &= 14+8e\blueD{-8e} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $8e$ from each side.}}\\\\
e+4&=14 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
e+4\blueD{-4} &= 14\blueD{-4} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $4$ from each side.}}\\\\
e &=\greenD{10} &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$e = \greenD{    10   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $   c   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 6c+14&=-5c+4+9c\\\\
6c+14&=4+4c &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
6c+14 \blueD{-4c} &= 4+4c\blueD{-4c} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $4c$ from each side.}}\\\\
2c+14&=4 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
2c+14\blueD{-14} &= 4\blueD{-14} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $14$ from each side.}}\\\\
2c &=-10 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{2c}{\blueD{2}} &= \dfrac{-10}{\blueD{2}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $2$.}}\\\\
c &= \greenD{-5} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$c = \greenD{  -5     }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $    t   $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 16-2t&=t+9+4t\\\\
16-2t&=5t+9&&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
16-2t \blueD{-5t} &= 5t+9\blueD{-5t} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $5t$ from each side.}}\\\\
-7t+16&=9 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-7t+16\blueD{-16} &= 9\blueD{-16} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $16$ from each side.}}\\\\
-7t &=-7 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-7t}{\blueD{-7}} &= \dfrac{-7}{\blueD{-7}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-7$.}}\\\\
t &= \greenD{1} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ t= \greenD{      1 }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
We need to manipulate the equation to get $  p     $ by itself.

$\begin{align} 17-2p&=2p+5+2p\\\\
17-2p&=4p+5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
17-2p \blueD{-4p} &= 4p+5\blueD{-4p} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $4p$ from each side.}}\\\\
-6p+17&=5 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
-6p+17\blueD{-17} &= 5\blueD{-17} &&\small\gray{\text{Subtract $17$ from each side.}}\\\\
-6p &=-12 &&\small\gray{\text{Combine like terms.}}\\\\
\dfrac{-6p}{\blueD{-6}} &= \dfrac{-12}{\blueD{-6}} &&\small\gray{\text{Divide each side by $-6$.}}\\\\
p &= \greenD{2} &&\small\gray{\text{Simplify.}}
\end{align}$

**The answer:**

>$ p= \greenD{    2   }~~~~~~~~$ [[☃ explanation 1]]
